zh.<HALDENBY, Yorkshire, England>: 英國英格蘭越可爾哈爾登比
zh.Snaith, East Riding of Yorkshire Unitary: 英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁史乃斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Guy D’Awnay於1453年出生在英國越可爾考維其可時，他的父親John Dawney29歲，母親Lady Agnes Radcliffe26歲。他於1484年與Lady Joan Darrell在英國越可爾史乃斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1522年8月17日在他的家鄉享年69歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁史乃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Guy D’Awnay于1453年出生在英国越可爾考維其可时，他的父亲John Dawney29岁，母亲Lady Agnes Radcliffe26岁。他于1484年与Lady Joan Darrell在英国越可爾史乃斯结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1522年8月17日在他的家乡享年69岁，埋葬在英国英格兰越可爾 易亞斯特 利丁史乃斯。

nameMeanings.name: D’Awnay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from one of the many places in northern France named from an alder grove (Old French aulnai, aulnoi, latinized as alnetum), such as Annay (Pas-de-Calais), Aulnay (Aube, Marne, Calvados), Aulnois (Aisne), and Aunay (Calvados, Eure-et-Loire, Seine-et-Oise). With fused preposition, Anglo-Norman French d'Aunai became Dawnay, Dawney and (with loss of the final syllable) Dawn. Laney is another form of this surname with fused definite article. See also Dando , from Anglo-Norman French d'Aunou, with which d'Aunai may sometimes have been confused.

variants.name: Laney
meanings: Irish: probably a shortened and altered form of   McElhinney  .

English and Irish: habitational name introduced by the Normans originally deriving from any of the many places in northern France called L'Aunai (from Latin alnetum ‘alder grove’). It may also have developed to Delaney .
Variant of   Laine   2, a surname of French origin.

variants.name: Dawn
meanings: English (Derbyshire and Nottinghamshire): of Norman origin, probably a clipped form of Dawnay or Dawney (compare Dana ), habitational names from one of the many places in northern France named for an alder grove (Old French aulnai, aulnoi, Latinized as alnetum). With fused preposition d(e), Anglo-Norman d'Aunai became Dawnay, Dawney and (with loss of the final syllable) Dawn. Laney is another form of this surname with fused definite article. See also Dando , from Anglo-Norman d'Aunou, with which d'Aunai may sometimes have been confused.

English: from Middle English Dawen, a variant of Dawes with the weak declension genitival suffix -(e)n (Old English -an) instead of the standard Middle English -es. The usage is mainly restricted to West Midlands dialects of Middle English.

variants.name: Doney
meanings: English (Cornwall): of uncertain origin. There is a Richard Donowe recorded in 1451-64 as a tenant in Manuels, Saint Columb Minor, whose surname appears to be a pet form of an unidentified personal name with the Cornish hypocoristic suffix -ow. Formally this might have become Doney but Donowe itself seems not to have survived, unless it was altered to Downey . However, there is no evidence to suggest any connection of this west Cornwall surname with Doney in central and east Cornwall (Bodmin and Quethiock). Alternatively, perhaps it is a variant of the Devon name Downey , and with the main vowel shortened and lowered, but evidence for this is lacking.
Americanized form of French Canadian   Daunais   or   Donais  .

variants.name: Dando
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name for someone from Aunou-le-Faucon in Orne, Normandy, with fused preposition. Aunou was named for an alder grove, from Latin alnus ‘alder’ + the Gaulish suffix -avum. In medieval England d'Aunou developed an intrusive -d-, giving Dando. The surname was sometimes confused with d'Aunay or d'Auney (see Delaney ).

nameMeanings.name: Guy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of a compound name starting with witu ‘wood’ or wīt ‘wide’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England. In Old French initial w- regularly became gu-. The usual Norman forms of the name were Gy or Guido. In medieval Latin the same name is found as Wido. It was a popular name among the Normans, enhanced no doubt by the romance of Guy of Warwick, recounting the exploits of a folk hero of the Crusades.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Guy D’Awnay was born in 1453, in Cowick, Yorkshire, England, his father, John Dawney, was 29 and his mother, Lady Agnes Radcliffe, was 26. He married Lady Joan Darrell in 1484, in Snaith, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 17 August 1522, in his hometown, at the age of 69, and was buried in Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom.

fr.Snaith, East Riding of Yorkshire Unitary: Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Guy D’Awnay est né en 1453, à Cowick, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, John Dawney, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Lady Agnes Radcliffe, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Lady Joan Darrell en 1484, à Snaith, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 17 août 1522, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.Snaith, East Riding of Yorkshire Unitary: Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Guy D’Awnay 1453 in Cowick, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Dawney 29 und seine Mutter Lady Agnes Radcliffe 26. Er hat 1484 in Snaith, Yorkshire, England Lady Joan Darrell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 17. August 1522 im Alter von 69 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, England, United Kingdom begraben.

Lui sposato Lady Joan Darrell nel 1484, nel Snaith, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 17 agosto 1522, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Snaith, Yorkshire East Riding, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Guy D’Awnayが1453年にイギリスヨークシュアイアコウィックで生まれた時，父John Dawneyは29歳，母Lady Agnes Radcliffeは26歳であった。1484年にイギリスヨークシュアイアスナイスでLady Joan Darrellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1522年8月17日に故郷で69歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスヨークシュアイア エアスト リディングスナイスにて埋葬された。

ko.Snaith, East Riding of Yorkshire Unitary: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스나이스
ko.lifeSummary: Sir Guy D’Awnay(이)가 1453년 영국 요르크실르 코윅에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Dawney(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Agnes Radcliffe(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1484년 영국 요르크실르 스나이스에서 Lady Joan Darrell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1522년 8월 17일 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 스나이스에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Lady Joan Darrell em 1484, em Snaith, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. ru.Snaith, East Riding of Yorkshire Unitary: снеит, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Guy D’Awnay родился в 1453 г. в местности коуикк, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Dawney было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes Radcliffe, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Joan Darrell в 1484 г. в местности снеит, еркшир, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 17 августа 1522 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности снеит, еркшир эаст ридинг, Англия, Великобритания.
